perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement:‐ ሺaሻ " Fintricity " means Fintricity and Fintricity Affiliates. ሺbሻ " FINTRICITY Affiliate " means any entity, whether incorporated or not, over which FINTRICITY has Control.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement:‐ ሺaሻ " Fintricity " means Fintricity and Fintricity Affiliates. ሺbሻ " FINTRICITY Affiliate " means any entity, whether incorporated or not, over which FINTRICITY believes it has Control.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'has Control' to 'believes it has Control' introduces subjectivity and contradicts the established legal definition of control.  Control, as defined in other sections of the agreement, relies on objective factors. The change can impact how affiliate status is determined, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 1159 defines control based on the ability to direct affairs through ownership or rights. Replacing 'has Control' with 'believes it has Control' makes the determination of affiliate status subjective, which undermines the objective legal standard of 'control' defined in the Act and the agreement itself, potentially leading to disagreement about which entities are covered by the NDA.

perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'FINTRICITY Affiliate' based on objective 'Control.' The changed text replaces 'has Control' with 'believes it has Control,' introducing subjectivity. This contradicts the requirement for objective determination of affiliate status, potentially leading to disputes over which entities are covered by the agreement. The Law Explanation supports that section 1159 defines control based on the ability to direct affairs through ownership or rights. Therefore, the change undermines the objective legal standard of 'control' and creates a legal contradiction as it deviates from legally recognized definitions making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the provision of Confidential Informatio n hereunder and any discussions held in connectio n with the Business Purpose sh all not prevent e i t h e r p a r t y f r o m p u r s u i n g s i m i l a r o r o t h e r d i s c u s s i o n s w i t h t h ird parties provided that no breach of this Agreement is so occasioned or o blige that party to take, continue or forego any action relating to the Business Purpose. Any estimates, forecasts or similar material provided by either par ty to the other shall not constitute any commitments.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that the provision of Confidential Informatio n hereunder and any discussions held in connectio n with the Business Purpose sh all not prevent e i t h e r p a r t y f r o m p u r s u i n g s i m i l a r o r o t h e r d i s c u s s i o n s w i t h t h ird parties, but any breach of confidentiality during those discussions will not be considered a breach of this Agreement. Any estimates, forecasts or similar material provided by either par ty to the other shall not constitute any commitments.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the core purpose of the NDA, which is to protect confidential information. By stating that breaches of confidentiality during similar discussions with third parties are not breaches of the agreement, it renders the NDA ineffective in those scenarios.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Law of Confidence, a principle of common law in the UK, establishes a legal duty to keep information confidential when it is imparted in circumstances importing an obligation of confidence. This modification seeks to waive the obligation of confidence during the discussion with third parties, directly conflicting with the fundamental tenets of the Law of Confidence which this NDA seeks to uphold. If a court finds that a party has waived their right to confidentiality, it would be very hard to show a breach of the law of confidence.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a significant contradiction to the NDA's purpose. The original text ensures that pursuing similar discussions with third parties does not excuse breaches of the agreement, while the changed text explicitly states that breaches of confidentiality during these discussions will not be considered a breach. This directly undermines the NDA's core function of protecting confidential information, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation. Although the scraped snippets are unhelpful, the contradiction is clear: The altered clause provides a loophole where confidentiality breaches with third parties are exempt, effectively nullifying the NDA's protections in those specific scenarios. This falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction, where contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable, because 'breach of confidentiality' should be a 'breach of contract' in order to be enforceable. By stating that a breach is NOT a breach of contract, the contract now defeats its own purpose.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the completion or termination of the Business Purpose, eac h recipient shall promptly deliver up to the disclosing party all Materials supplied by the disclosing party incorporating any Confidential Information of that party and all copies thereof and destroy or erase any Confidential Informatio n contained in any materials and documentation prepared by or on behalf of the recipient or recorded in any memory device, unless retention is deemed necessary by the Receiving Party's legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity and potential conflict by allowing the Receiving Party's legal counsel to unilaterally decide if retention of confidential information is 'necessary'. This weakens the disclosing party's control over its confidential information upon termination.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 3 defines the unlawful acquisition, use, and disclosure of trade secrets. By allowing the Receiving Party's counsel to unilaterally decide on retention, this amendment introduces the risk that the Receiving Party may unlawfully acquire, use or disclose trade secrets after the termination of the NDA. This conflicts with the rights afforded to the trade secret holder under the Act and makes enforcement challenging due to the subjective criterion for retention.

perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a legal contradiction. The original text mandates the destruction or erasure of confidential information upon termination. The altered text adds an exception: retention is permissible if deemed necessary by the Receiving Party's legal counsel. This creates ambiguity and allows for potential misuse of confidential information post-termination, undermining the disclosing party's control and potentially conflicting with trade secret regulations. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both highlight this weakening of control and potential for unlawful use. Although Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1 makes it clear that the Trade Secrets (Enforcement, etc.) Regulations 2018 are relevant. By potentially allowing retention of trade secrets after termination, the contract risks violating regulations regarding unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure, as suggested by the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that, during the term of this agreement and for a period of 48 months thereafter, the Company shall not, whether by itself, its officers, personnel, contractors or agents of the Company or whether by a Company A f f i l i a t e o r t h e o f f i c e r s , p e r s o n n e l , c o n t r a c t o r s o r a g e n t s o f that Company Affiliates, solicit or seek to solicit orders from any customer of Fintricity for services similar to the services which Fintricity provides to t hat customer without the prior written consent of Fintricity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that, during the term of this agreement and for a period of 48 months thereafter, the Company shall not, whether by itself, its officers, personnel, contractors or agents of the Company or whether by a Company A f f i l i a t e o r t h e o f f i c e r s , p e r s o n n e l , c o n t r a c t o r s o r a g e n t s o f that Company Affiliates, solicit or seek to solicit orders from any customer of Fintricity for services that Fintricity deems similar to the services which Fintricity provides to t hat customer without the prior written consent of Fintricity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'similar to the services' to 'that Fintricity deems similar to the services' grants Fintricity unilateral and subjective power to define the scope of the non-solicitation clause. This makes the restriction overly broad and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restraint of Trade doctrine requires that any restriction on a party's ability to conduct business be reasonable in scope and necessary to protect legitimate business interests. By giving Fintricity the sole discretion to define 'similar services,' the amended clause creates a potentially unreasonable and overly broad restriction on the Company's ability to compete, thus violating the principle of Restraint of Trade. This could lead a court to deem the entire clause unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: restraint of trade | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you restraint of trade A restraint of trade is any activity that tends to limit a party's ability to enter into transactions. The term is most commonly used in the context of government antitrust regulation. For example, federally, 15 U.S.C. § 1 prohibits “[e]very contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce among the several States, or with foreign nations.” For example, in American Needle, Inc. v. Nat’l Football League , the U.S. Supreme Court held that licensing activities for individual teams’ intellectual property conducted through a corporation separate from the teams with its own management [i.e. the NFL] fell within a possible restraint of trade covered under 15 U.S.C. § 1. Many states also have their own local restraints on anti-competitive business activity. For example, Massachusetts General Law Ch. 93 § 4 states “[e]very contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce in the commonwealth shall be unlawful.” [Last reviewed in April of 2025 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities unfair competition wex definitions Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a legal contradiction by granting Fintricity unilateral power to define 'similar services' within the non-solicitation clause. This potentially violates the Restraint of Trade doctrine, which requires restrictions on a party's ability to conduct business to be reasonable. The original text used a more objective standard ('similar to the services'), while the changed text allows Fintricity's subjective determination to dictate the scope, potentially creating an overly broad and unreasonable restriction. The scraped law snippet defines 'restraint of trade' as 'any activity that tends to limit a party's ability to enter into transactions.' By giving Fintricity broad discretion, the modified clause risks unduly limiting the Company's ability to compete, thus contradicting the principle against restraint of trade.
